entity.label: Shemaiah
entity.subtitle: Male
entity.description: The eldest son of Obed-edom (1 Chr. 26:4-8).

nodes.label: Shemaiah
nodes.description: The eldest son of Obed-edom (1 Chr. 26:4-8).

nodes.label: Elihu
nodes.description: One of the family of Obed-edom, who were appointed porters of the temple under David (1 Chr. 26:7).

nodes.label: Rephael
nodes.description: Healed of God, one of Shemaiah’s sons. He and his brethren, on account of their “strength for service,” formed one of the divisions of the temple porters (1 Chr. 26:7, 8).

nodes.label: Othni
nodes.description: A lion of Jehovah, a son of Shemaiah, and one of the temple porters in the time of David (1 Chr. 26:7). He was a “mighty man of valour.”

nodes.label: Ammiel
nodes.description: One of the sons of Obed-edom the Levite (1 Chr. 26:5).

nodes.label: Jehozabad
nodes.description: The son of Obed-edom (1 Chr. 26:4), one of the Levite porters.

nodes.label: Joah
nodes.description: One of the sons of Obed-edom (1 Chr. 26:4), a Korhite porter.

nodes.label: Nethaneel
nodes.description: A temple porter, of the family of the Korhites (1 Chr. 26:4).

nodes.label: Peulthai
nodes.description: Wages of the Lord, one of the sons of Obed-edom, a Levite porter (1 Chr. 26:5).

nodes.label: Sacar
nodes.description: A son of Obed-edom the Gittite, and a temple porter (1 Chr. 26:4).

verses.text: Moreover the sons of Obededom were, Shemaiah the firstborn, Jehozabad the second, Joah the third, and Sacar the fourth, and Nethaneel the fifth,
Also unto Shemaiah his son were sons born, that ruled throughout the house of their father: for they were mighty men of valour.
The sons of Shemaiah; Othni, and Rephael, and Obed, Elzabad, whose brethren were strong men, Elihu, and Semachiah.
